ddls:
CREATE TABLE "batches" (
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each batch */,
  "batch_no" TEXT /* Batch number of the medication */,
  "drug_id" INTEGER /* ID of the drug associated with the batch */,
  "expiry_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the batch */,
  "manufacture_date" TEXT /* Manufacturing date of the batch */,
  "batch_status" TEXT /* Status of the batch (e.g., active, inactive, expired) */,
  "batch_size" INTEGER /* Total quantity of the batch */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier for the batch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("batch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batches_drug_id FOREIGN KEY ("drug_id") REFERENCES drugs ("drug_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "drugs" (
  "drug_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each drug */,
  "drug_name" TEXT /* Name of the drug */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the drug */,
  "dosage" TEXT /* Dosage instructions for the drug */,
  "drug_type" TEXT /* Type of the drug (e.g., tablet, capsule, liquid) */,
  "drug_composition" TEXT /* Composition of the drug */,
  "side_effects" TEXT /* Potential side effects of the drug */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("drug_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory entry */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch in the inventory */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse where the batch is stored */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the batch in the inventory */,
  "storage_conditions" TEXT /* Storage conditions for the batch (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES batches ("batch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "warehouses" (
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each warehouse */,
  "warehouse_name" TEXT /* Name of the warehouse */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the warehouse */,
  "warehouse_capacity" INTEGER /* Maximum capacity of the warehouse */,
  "warehouse_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the manager for the warehouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("warehouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "batch_warehouse" (
  "batch_warehouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each batch-warehouse entry */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch */,
  "warehouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the warehouse */,
  "storage_date" TEXT /* Date when the batch was stored in the warehouse */,
  "retrieval_date" TEXT /* Date when the batch was retrieved from the warehouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("batch_warehouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batch_warehouse_warehouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("warehouse_id") REFERENCES warehouses ("warehouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batch_warehouse_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES batches ("batch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, warehouse manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., production, quality control) */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the batch */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the batch was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, update) */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES batches ("batch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch triggering the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., expiration, low quantity) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES batches ("batch_id")
)
